strategicBrief.text: SINGLE-MARKET DEPENDENCY: North America now 45% of revenue (was 37%). If ADF were a stock, an analyst would flag 'geographic concentration risk.' Target: no zone >35% within 3 years through active diversification.

PORTFOLIO HEALTH: CRITICAL: 1,007 SKUs but only ~200 needed for 90% of revenue. 590 Product Families expose the redundancy — multiple SKUs per family with negligible incremental revenue. SKU count should be a KPI with downward target.

GROWTH QUALITY CONCERN: Revenue +5.8% looks healthy. But: $/KG declining, new customers buying at lower prices, Dry segment shrinking. Top-line growth is masking structural margin pressure. Request margin-level reporting from CFO.
THE FROZEN BET IS WINNING: Frozen grew +$2.85M (+12%) while Dry fell -$435K (-2.4%). Every incremental CapEx dollar should flow toward frozen capacity. The dry portfolio should shrink by design.

disc.category: GROWTH IS CONCENTRATED: 🚀 Fast Growth PFs are few but large — they're driving ADF's top-line. If these PFs stall (capacity constraints, competitive response), ADF's growth evaporates. Protect them.

DECLINING + FAST DECLINE PFs: MARGIN DRAIN: Declining PFs typically show volume erosion + pricing pressure simultaneously. Every quarter these PFs remain in the portfolio, they consume capacity that could serve growth PFs.

NEW PFs NEED VELOCITY GATES: New PFs should hit $50K revenue within 6 months or face discontinuation review. Too many new products limp along at $5-10K/year indefinitely, consuming packaging inventory and planning attention.
DISCONTINUED PFs: RECLAIM IMMEDIATELY: Any PF with zero CY revenue that still has packaging inventory or production templates occupying ERP should be fully archived. Clean the system.

disc.productFamily: ▸ ACTION [CEO + Category Heads] [30 DAYS]: IMPLEMENT VELOCITY GATES: New PFs must hit $50K/6 months. Declining PFs get 2-quarter recovery window. Fast Decline = immediate exit review.
